other_observers: John Stulp, Jensen Stulp, Janeal Thompson, Dotti Russell; original
specific_location: farm yard 5 miles south of Lamar on Hwy 287 and a quarter of mile to the west
description: similar in size to a Hermit Thrush - at first glance that's what I thought it was. -overall appearance reddish /rufous color with gray rump and red/rufous stripes across back; -reddish rufous triangular shaped face patch with top edge connected behind eye with very narrow black line; - three white patches on throat - one in center just under bill and a white "mustache" -bicolored bill - bill gray on top with lower yellow -gray head with hint of rufous crown;  -red/rufous wings with narrow whitish wing bars;  -reddish/rufous tail; -dark streaks on white flanks -dark rufous streaking and spots on white breast; -white lower under belly -pink legsBehaviors: -similar feeding action to a towhee (scratching through fallen leaves) -was uncovering and eating sunflower seeds on ground that had fallen from feeder -stayed more under brush than in the openCall: not heardPlumage: Red Taiga
photographer: Photographer: Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp
